sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: TODO: Articulate the problem. Consider: AI coding agents (Copilot, Cursor, Claude) need structured access to codebase metadata — function purposes, staleness status, dependency graphs, and documentation — so they can generate context-aware code and maintain documentation. Without an MCP interface, agents must parse raw source files or rely on incomplete prompts.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
TODO: Write user stories. Suggestions:
- As an AI agent I want to search the knowledge graph so I can find relevant functions before generating code
- As an AI agent I want to check staleness so I can prioritize which docs to update
- As an AI agent I want to read the dependency graph so I can understand what a function produces/consumes
- As an AI agent I want to mark nodes as verified so my documentation reviews are recorded
- As an AI agent I want to write DocJSON files so I can create documentation as part of my workflow
- As a developer I want the MCP server to work with VS Code so I can use cortex tools in my editor

sections.heading: Requirements
sections.content:
TODO: Prioritize and finalize requirements. Current implementation provides 16 MCP tools:
- cortex_build: Rebuild the index
- cortex_render: Multi-level detail rendering (level_0 through level_3); levels 1 and 2 append inline staleness badges ([CODE_STALE], [STRUCTURAL_DRIFT], etc.) for non-CLEAN nodes — CLEAN/VERIFIED nodes are annotation-free
- cortex_list: Filtered node listing with parent_id and location scoping
- cortex_graph: Neighborhood traversal with depth and direction
- cortex_search: 3-stage full-text search (FTS5 → LIKE-AND → LIKE-OR)
- cortex_check: Staleness detection; default (verbose=False) returns summary line + CONTENT_STALE sections only; verbose=True returns full problem-node table
- cortex_history: Change timeline with stale-window annotations
- cortex_mark_clean: Agent verification recording
- cortex_source: Raw source body lookup by node ID
- cortex_write_doc: DocJSON file creation/update
- cortex_add_link: Edge creation between doc sections and code nodes
- cortex_update_section: Patch a single section's content/heading in a DocJSON file
- cortex_read_doc: DocJSON rendering to markdown, with optional section slug filter
- cortex_list_undocumented: Find nodes without documentation
- cortex_workflow_list: List dFlow workflows/tasks/test_workflows with module and role filters, paginated, with cortex node ID cross-references
- cortex_workflow_detail: Show ordered steps and metadata for a single workflow; verbose mode includes purpose, inputs, outputs, critical on each step; test_workflow functions include coverage targets

sections.heading: Non-Goals
sections.content:
TODO: Confirm non-goals. Likely candidates:
- Multi-user concurrent access (single-agent usage pattern)
- Real-time push notifications (MCP is request/response)
- Code execution or testing through MCP (use cortex_build / cortex test CLI instead)
- Authentication (local stdio transport only)

sections.heading: Icebox / Future Ideas
sections.content:
TODO: Capture stretch goals. Ideas from the codebase:
- cortex_refactor: suggest refactoring based on dependency analysis
- cortex_test_scope: return tests that cover a specific node (for agent-driven TDD)
- cortex_diff: compare two history checkpoints
- SSE transport for remote MCP access (currently stdio only)
